english_name: The Epistle of Paul the Apostle to the Hebrews

verses.latin_vulgate: Timeamus ergo ne forte relicta pollicitatione introëundi in requiem ejus, existimetur aliquis ex vobis deesse.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: φοβηθῶμεν οὖν μήποτε καταλειπομένης ἐπαγγελίας εἰσελθεῖν εἰς τὴν κατάπαυσιν αὐτοῦ δοκῇ τις ἐξ ὑμῶν ὑστερηκέναι
verses.king_james: Let us therefore fear, lest, a promise being left *us* of entering into his rest, any of you should seem to come short of it.

verses.latin_vulgate: Etenim et nobis nuntiatum est, quemadmodum et illis: sed non profuit illis sermo auditus, non admistus fidei ex iis quæ audierunt.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ γάρ ἐσμεν εὐηγγελισμένοι καθάπερ κἀκεῖνοι ἀλλ᾽ οὐκ ὠφέλησεν ὁ λόγος τῆς ἀκοῆς ἐκείνους μὴ συγκεκραμένος τῇ πίστει τοῖς ἀκούσασιν
verses.king_james: For unto us was the gospel preached, as well as unto them: but the word preached did not profit them, not being mixed with faith in them that heard *it*.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ingrediemur enim in requiem, qui credidimus: quemadmodum dixit: Sicut juravi in ira mea: Si introibunt in requiem meam: et quidem operibus ab institutione mundi perfectis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἰσερχόμεθα γὰρ εἰς τὴν κατάπαυσιν οἱ πιστεύσαντες καθὼς εἴρηκεν ὡς ὤμοσα ἐν τῇ ὀργῇ μου εἰ εἰσελεύσονται εἰς τὴν κατάπαυσίν μου καίτοι τῶν ἔργων ἀπὸ καταβολῆς κόσμου γενηθέντων
verses.king_james: For we which have believed do enter into rest, as he said, As I have sworn in my wrath, if they shall enter into my rest: although the works were finished from the foundation of the world.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixit enim in quodam loco de die septima sic: Et requievit Deus die septima ab omnibus operibus suis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἴρηκεν γάρ που περὶ τῆς ἑβδόμης οὕτως καὶ κατέπαυσεν ὁ θεὸς ἐν τῇ ἡμέρᾳ τῇ ἑβδόμῃ ἀπὸ πάντων τῶν ἔργων αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: For he spake in a certain place of the seventh *day* on this wise, And God did rest the seventh day from all his works.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et in isto rursum: Si introibunt in requiem meam.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐν τούτῳ πάλιν εἰ εἰσελεύσονται εἰς τὴν κατάπαυσίν μου
verses.king_james: And in this *place* again, If they shall enter into my rest.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quoniam ergo superest introire quosdam in illam, et ii, quibus prioribus annuntiatum est, non introierunt propter incredulitatem:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐπεὶ οὖν ἀπολείπεται τινὰς εἰσελθεῖν εἰς αὐτήν καὶ οἱ πρότερον εὐαγγελισθέντες οὐκ εἰσῆλθον δι᾽ ἀπείθειαν
verses.king_james: Seeing therefore it remaineth that some must enter therein, and they to whom it was first preached entered not in because of unbelief:

verses.latin_vulgate: iterum terminat diem quemdam, Hodie, in David dicendo, post tantum temporis, sicut supra dictum est: Hodie si vocem ejus audieritis, nolite obdurare corda vestra.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πάλιν τινὰ ὁρίζει ἡμέραν σήμερον ἐν δαβὶδ λέγων μετὰ τοσοῦτον χρόνον καθὼς εἴρηται, σήμερον ἐὰν τῆς φωνῆς αὐτοῦ ἀκούσητε μὴ σκληρύνητε τὰς καρδίας ὑμῶν
verses.king_james: Again, he limiteth a certain day, saying in David, To day, after so long a time; as it is said, To day if ye will hear his voice, harden not your hearts.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nam si eis Jesus requiem præstitisset, numquam de alia loqueretur, posthac, die.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἰ γὰρ αὐτοὺς ἰησοῦς κατέπαυσεν οὐκ ἂν περὶ ἄλλης ἐλάλει μετὰ ταῦτα ἡμέρας
verses.king_james: For if Jesus had given them rest, then would he not afterward have spoken of another day.

verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἄρα ἀπολείπεται σαββατισμὸς τῷ λαῷ τοῦ θεοῦ
verses.king_james: There remaineth therefore a rest to the people of God.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui enim ingressus est in requiem ejus, etiam ipse requievit ab operibus suis, sicut a suis Deus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ γὰρ εἰσελθὼν εἰς τὴν κατάπαυσιν αὐτοῦ καὶ αὐτὸς κατέπαυσεν ἀπὸ τῶν ἔργων αὐτοῦ ὥσπερ ἀπὸ τῶν ἰδίων ὁ θεός
verses.king_james: For he that is entered into his rest, he also hath ceased from his own works, as God *did* from his.

verses.latin_vulgate: Festinemus ergo ingredi in illam requiem: ut ne in idipsum quis incidat incredulitatis exemplum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: σπουδάσωμεν οὖν εἰσελθεῖν εἰς ἐκείνην τὴν κατάπαυσιν ἵνα μὴ ἐν τῷ αὐτῷ τις ὑποδείγματι πέσῃ τῆς ἀπειθείας
verses.king_james: Let us labour therefore to enter into that rest, lest any man fall after the same example of unbelief.

verses.latin_vulgate: Vivus est enim sermo Dei, et efficax et penetrabilior omni gladio ancipiti: et pertingens usque ad divisionem animæ ac spiritus: compagum quoque ac medullarum, et discretor cogitationum et intentionum cordis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ζῶν γὰρ ὁ λόγος τοῦ θεοῦ καὶ ἐνεργὴς καὶ τομώτερος ὑπὲρ πᾶσαν μάχαιραν δίστομον καὶ διϊκνούμενος ἄχρι μερισμοῦ ψυχῆς τε καὶ πνεύματος ἁρμῶν τὲ καὶ μυελῶν καὶ κριτικὸς ἐνθυμήσεων καὶ ἐννοιῶν καρδίας
verses.king_james: For the word of God *is* quick, and powerful, and sharper than any twoedged sword, piercing even to the dividing asunder of soul and spirit, and of the joints and marrow, and *is* a discerner of the thoughts and intents of the heart.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et non est ulla creatura invisibilis in conspectu ejus: omnia autem nuda et aperta sunt oculis ejus, ad quem nobis sermo.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ οὐκ ἔστιν κτίσις ἀφανὴς ἐνώπιον αὐτοῦ πάντα δὲ γυμνὰ καὶ τετραχηλισμένα τοῖς ὀφθαλμοῖς αὐτοῦ πρὸς ὃν ἡμῖν ὁ λόγος
verses.king_james: Neither is there any creature that is not manifest in his sight: but all things *are* naked and opened unto the eyes of him with whom we have to do.

verses.latin_vulgate: Habentes ergo pontificem magnum qui penetravit cælos, Jesum Filium Dei, teneamus confessionem.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἕχοντες οὖν ἀρχιερέα μέγαν διεληλυθότα τοὺς οὐρανούς ἰησοῦν τὸν υἱὸν τοῦ θεοῦ κρατῶμεν τῆς ὁμολογίας
verses.king_james: Seeing then that we have a great high priest, that is passed into the heavens, Jesus the Son of God, let us hold fast *our* profession.

verses.latin_vulgate: Non enim habemus pontificem qui non possit compati infirmitatibus nostris: tentatum autem per omnia pro similitudine absque peccato.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὐ γὰρ ἔχομεν ἀρχιερέα μὴ δυνάμενον συμπαθῆσαι ταῖς ἀσθενείαις ἡμῶν πεπειραμένον δὲ κατὰ πάντα καθ᾽ ὁμοιότητα χωρὶς ἁμαρτίας
verses.king_james: For we have not an high priest which cannot be touched with the feeling of our infirmities; but was in all points tempted like as *we are, yet* without sin.

verses.latin_vulgate: Adeamus ergo cum fiducia ad thronum gratiæ: ut misericordiam consequamur, et gratiam inveniamus in auxilio opportuno.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: προσερχώμεθα οὖν μετὰ παῤῥησίας τῷ θρόνῳ τῆς χάριτος ἵνα λάβωμεν ἔλεον, καὶ χάριν εὕρωμεν εἰς εὔκαιρον βοήθειαν
verses.king_james: Let us therefore come boldly unto the throne of grace, that we may obtain mercy, and find grace to help in time of need.
